name: TET2 CHIP patients on canakinumab had lower solid-tumor incidence
summary: A second CANTOS CHIP analysis supports disease-relevance for IL-1beta blockade in TET2 clonal hematopoiesis, but remains exploratory and not clone-control evidence.
statement: In a CANTOS genomic analysis, patients with TET2 mutations assigned canakinumab had the lowest incidence of non-hematological malignancy across cancer types and lower cumulative incidence of at least one malignancy than TET2-mutant patients assigned placebo.
population_or_model: CANTOS participants with clonal hematopoiesis mutation sequencing and malignancy follow-up
quantitative_note: The abstract reports lower cumulative malignancy incidence in TET2-mutant patients treated with canakinumab versus placebo but does not support a broad cancer-prevention claim.
caveats: The analysis is post hoc and exploratory.
The signal is specific to canakinumab, TET2 mutation status, and a high-risk CANTOS population.
This does not show reduction in mutation burden, clonal expansion, or general aging risk.
